name: Souvlaki with Tzaziki
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Lamb shoulder (or Sirloin tip, cut into 1 1/2-inch chunks)
recipeInstructions:
----------------------------------TZAZIKI----------------------------------
2 c Yogurt
- 2 Garlic cloves, minced
- 1/2 Cucumber, peeled and grated
- 3 tbsp Olive oil
- Salt (to taste)
Begin preparing the tzaziki a few hours before serving. The flavors need time to blend. Marinate the meat overnight to absorb the wine and garlic.
Souvlaki:
1. Place the meat in a large bowl.
2. Pour in the red wine and sprinkle minced garlic, dried oregano, and lemon strips among the cubes of meat.
3. Drizzle with olive oil. Cover tightly with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 12 hours or overnight.
4. About 30 minutes before serving, discard the marinade.
5. Thread the chunks of meat onto long skewers, brushing with a little olive oil. (Separate skewers can be used for peppers and tomatoes.)
6. Over medium coals, barbecue the souvlaki until cooked to your liking. Serve on a bed of rice and pass tzaziki for dipping. SERVES 4-6
Tzaziki:
1. Line a colander or sieve with cheesecloth (or gauze) and place over a bowl.
2. Pour in the yogurt and allow it to drain for about an hour.
3. After draining, transfer the yogurt into a small bowl.
4. Combine with grated cucumber and minced garlic. Cover tightly with plastic wrap and refrigerate until just before serving.
5. While the meat is barbecuing, drizzle the olive oil over the tzaziki and sprinkle salt to taste.
It should not be mixed; guests will stir it onto their souvlaki.
Makes: About 3 cups
